entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
When students are young, we want them to come to know God through his stories. In Grade 2, students read the stories directly from the Bible, which they were introduced to in Grade 1. Lesson plans also include occasional artwork illustrating the story read and suggested prep selections for teachers from resources such as the Bible Story Handbook, which provides biblical context, helps with interpretational issues, and points out mistakes to avoid when reading specific Bible passages.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Bible: Grade 2

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: Students in Grade 2 may take this course, which reads straight from the Bible, or First Bible Stories. They may also combine with Grades 3-6 Bible if they are familiar with OT narratives.

rows.label: Old Testament: Grade 2
rows.books: ESV Large Print Bible||God's Bible Timeline: The Big Book of Biblical History||The Bible Story Handbook

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Old Testament: Grade 2
☐ Read the introduction (p.13-30) in The Bible Story Handbook that includes the following sections: "Why Do We Teach Bible Stories?", "Is There a Right Way or a Wrong Way to Use Bible Stories?" & "The Big Picture of the Bible."

☐ Watch Dr. John Walton's video about using the Handbook. The link can be found in Quick Links below. 

☐ You may want to watch the overview videos from The Bible Project on Genesis, Exodus, Joshua, Judges, 1 Kings, Esther, Daniel, and Jonah. They help you see the major themes from the different Bible books. The link can be found in Quick Links below.

books.title: God's Bible Timeline: The Big Book of Biblical History

blocks.text:
• Connect to previous lessons by asking students to recall where they left off. 

• If students have difficulty recalling, teachers might try showing them a picture from the reading, sharing something the teacher remembers, or prompting them with the first sentence of the last reading. This will often refresh their memory enough to allow them to recall.

• If students still cannot recall anything from the last reading, teachers might consider continuing anyway (with a shorter reading this time) or repeating the previous lesson, depending on the reading and the nature of the book.

• Read the chapter or selection aloud. When reading to pre-readers, point to the words as you read, even if it seems they aren't paying attention.

• Move at the students' pace during the lesson, rather than feeling that a particular number of pages must be completed. It is vitally important that teachers not overload students with more material than they can absorb in one sitting.

• Students who have difficulty sitting still or listening may benefit from a fidget of their choice, such as a piece of putty or clay or an afghan; flexible positioning, such as a wobble seat or inverted sitting; or sensory modulation, such as a weighted vest or blanket or adjustment of light, temperature, or sound.

• Students with regular difficulty engaging often benefit from having a picture to associate with the story WHILE listening. Feel free to view the artwork during the reading rather than after the narration.

• Have students retell the passage orally.

• Alternatively, learners may use words, pictures, Legos, dolls, etc., to process and convey ideas in the manner most natural to them.  

• Teachers may take turns to model.

• Narration is a complex skill requiring the coordination of several parts of the brain. If a student struggles to narrate in general, with a specific subject or book, or on one day but not others, then this is an opportunity to learn. Observe when they struggle with this or any other activity today. Notice facial expressions and body language; listen to what they say about why it is hard or how they feel about it. Take note of these observations for later, tell them that you see how hard they are working, and then offer whatever support they need to end on a positive note. If necessary, take a break for the day.
• Optional: Share a piece of fine art that illustrates the story.
• Give students an opportunity to share their thoughts on the lesson and any connections made.

lessons.title: 20m Old Testament: Grade 2 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
God Created Light and the World Around Us 
⍞ Materials: Bible Story Handbook

PREP: If you haven't read the Introduction section on p.13-30, please do that. Prior to this lesson, read lessons 1-2, p.33-37. 

➜ INTRO
This year we will be reading true stories from the Bible that show us who God is and what he is like. This is his story of how he created a people with whom he could dwell and have a relationship.  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Genesis 1:1-19 (Stop after several verses to have student narrate.)

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
Talk about what was created each day.
∞ Art Print Link: Creation Window

lessons.title: 20m Old Testament: Grade 2 - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
Noah 
⍞ Materials: Bible Story Handbook, God's Bible Timeline

➜ NOTE 
Various times throughout the year, we have the students look at God's Bible Timeline as a way to recap the previous lessons. You can read the descriptions of the events or just quickly go through them. Not all of the events are covered in their readings. Feel free to skip those or briefly talk about them. 

PREP: Read lesson 7 p.47-49 

➜ RECAP
God's Bible Timeline p.12-13 (top row)
We will look at a timeline that shows the events we have read about so far. Try to guess what story the picture is illustrating. 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
Genesis 7:1-16

lessons.title: 20m Old Testament: Grade 2 - Lesson 87
lessons.body:
Elisha and the Widow's Oil; Elisha and the Shunammite 
⍞ Materials: Bible Story Handbook

PREP: Read lessons 75-76 p.208-211  

➜ INTRO
We will be reading about different men called prophets that God sent to bring the people messages from God. Sometimes the prophets spoke to one person, such as a king, and other times the prophets would preach to a group of people. 

➜ RECAP
What did you read about Elijah going up to Heaven last time?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS 
2 Kings 4:1-7; 2 Kings 4:8-37

terms.content:
· Tell about the Fall of Adam and Eve OR tell about God saving Noah from the Flood.

· Tell about Joseph saving his family during the famine OR Abraham's servant finding a wife for Isaac.

· Tell about Moses going against Pharaoh OR tell about one of the Judges (Ehud, Deborah, Gideon, Samson).

· Tell about Moses and the Ten Commandments OR tell about the making of the tabernacle.

· Tell about Saul anointed king OR tell about David and Goliath.

· Tell a story about Elijah or Elisha OR tell about the building of the temple.
